task_completion.description: Import the regional topographic dataset from the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an import dialog, file browser, or application workspace showing the file 'C:\workspace\data\regional_topo.xyz' being loaded, ideally with settings for space-delimited 'PointNumber X Y Z' format.

task_completion.description: Define a 300m x 300m rectangular project boundary using the specified coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a boundary creation tool, coordinate input dialog, or drawn rectangle showing the coordinates: X from 5000.00 to 5300.00 and Y from 4900.00 to 5200.00.

task_completion.description: Delete all survey points located outside the defined boundary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a selection of points outside the boundary being deleted, or the workspace updating to show only the points strictly within the 300m x 300m rectangular area.

task_completion.description: Export the remaining interior points to the specified .xyz file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an export dialog or save prompt showing the remaining points being saved to 'C:\workspace\data\site_aoi_points.xyz'.

task_completion.description: Save the project to the specified .tcp file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' dialog or the application title bar indicating the project is being saved to 'C:\workspace\data\site_aoi.tcp'.

integrity.description: The agent used a topography or CAD application GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent is interacting with a graphical application (e.g., TopoCal or similar) rather than writing scripts in a terminal or manually editing files in a text editor.

integrity.description: The points were cropped using spatial tools within the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent used the application's selection or boundary tools to remove points, rather than manually deleting lines in a text editor.

integrity.description: The exported points and saved project were generated via the application's built-in functions.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the application's native export and save dialogs being used to create the .xyz and .tcp files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input file: C:\workspace\data\regional_topo.xyz. Expected points file: C:\workspace\data\site_aoi_points.xyz. Expected project file: C:\workspace\data\site_aoi.tcp. Boundary bounds: x_min=5000.0, x_max=5300.0, y_min=4900.0, y_max=5200.0.
